evidence: The problem asks to 'cover the interval [y] by residue classes a_p (mod p), one for each prime p <= x' — a covering-by-residue-classes (covering system) question.

Docstring: 'Suppose that a large sieve process leaves a set of quadratic size. Is that set quadratic?' — the inverse large sieve problem of [GH14].
The hypothesis is a small-sumset condition '|A + A| <= K|A|' on A in F_2^n.
The file states the Polynomial Freiman-Ruzsa conjecture (Marton's conjecture), the polynomial-bounds strengthening of the Freiman-Ruzsa inverse sumset theorem, solved in [GGM25].
The statement asks whether the 10-fold iterated sumset 10A of a dense set A in F_2^n contains a coset of a large subspace.
A structure-of-iterated-sumsets question in F_2^n from Green's open problem list, squarely within additive combinatorics (AMS 5 11).
The problem asks for the largest coset (or arithmetic progression) guaranteed to be contained in the sumset A + A of a set of density alpha.
The problem asks for the largest coset (or arithmetic progression) guaranteed to be contained in the sumset A + A of a set of density alpha.

The problem asks for the largest coset (or arithmetic progression) guaranteed to be contained in the sumset A + A of a set of density alpha.
Bound on 'the largest length of an arithmetic progression guaranteed to be contained in A+A' [Gr02, 'Arithmetic progressions in sumsets'].
Bound on 'the largest length of an arithmetic progression guaranteed to be contained in A+A' [Ruz91, 'Arithmetic progressions in sumsets'].
The question is whether the sumset 2A = A + A of a set with an additive complement of size K contains a coset of small codimension.
The question is whether the sumset 2A = A + A of a set with an additive complement of size K contains a coset of small codimension.

The hypothesis and conclusion are stated in terms of the (infinite-dimensional product) Gaussian measure gamma with gamma(K) >= 0.99, per Talagrand's 'Are All Sets of Positive Measure Essentially Convex?'.
The question asks whether 10K (resp. 2K) contains a compact convex set C of Gaussian measure >= 0.01.

The hypothesis and conclusion are stated in terms of the (infinite-dimensional product) Gaussian measure gamma with gamma(K) >= 0.99, per Talagrand's 'Are All Sets of Positive Measure Essentially Convex?'.
The question asks whether 10K (resp. 2K) contains a compact convex set C of Gaussian measure >= 0.01.
The problem concerns spaces Phi(G) and Phi'(G) of functions on a finite abelian group G (the variants take G = Z/3Z).
Phi(G) is a 'generalised convolution algebra' as considered by Conlon-Fox-Zhao, built from kernels averaging f_1 f_2 f_3 over x_1+x_2+x_3 = g.
